title: Supply Chain Inventory Analyst
description: **Supply Chain Inventory Analyst \- Ireland, Dublin \- 12 Month Contract** Located near Dublin, our global pharmaceutical client is seeking a Supply Chain Inventory Analyst to join their state of the art manufacturing facility. The company focuses on investing in research and development to drive scientific innovation and deliver life changing medicines. With thousands of employees worldwide and continued growth across their network, this is an excellent opportunity to join a leading organisation shaping the future of healthcare. **What You’ll Do** * Track inventory depletion rates of “old” source stock to ensure stakeholders are aware of timing and preparations needed to transact “new” stock. * Track the shipment of Finished Goods and coordinate with Logistics/Quality to release stock cooincides with the required system changes. * Monitor FEFO (First Expired, First Out) logic to minimise write\-offs during the transition. Flag short\-dated stock immediately. * Maintain the Inventory Tracker Dashboard (Excel) to provide real\-time visibility into the transition progress. **What you’ll need:** * Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business, or Life Sciences. * Extensive experience in Supply Chain or Inventory Management (required), specifically within the Pharmaceutical or Medical Device industry (preferred). * Technical Skills such as: ERP Proficiency, Experienced user of SAP or similar, Excel: Advanced (VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, Power Query) are mandatory for data reconciliation. **Apply now to become a part of this life changing project!**

title: Tax Associate
description: **Tax Associate / Part‑Qualified** **Location:** Swords, Co. Dublin **Full time, Permanent position** **The Company** Our client is a rapidly growing, independent accountancy and tax advisory firm with a strong presence in Ireland and the UK. The firm provides high‑quality tax and corporate services to a diverse client base and is committed to fostering a supportive and inclusive environment where ambitious professionals can thrive and grow. varied client portfolio, and support day‑to‑day tax compliance and advisory work within a collaborative team. **Responsibilities** * Support preparation and filing of tax returns (income tax, corporation tax, VAT and related submissions) across a range of clients * Assist with tax compliance tasks including computations, review of client data, and ensuring timely submissions to authorities * Liaise with clients to gather necessary financial information and documentation * Help identify tax‑efficient solutions and highlight issues for senior team members * Work collaboratively with colleagues to deliver high‑quality client service * Maintain up‑to‑date knowledge of tax legislation and assist with basic research or enquiries **Requirements** * Part‑qualified (e.g. AITI/TMITI/CTA studies in progress) or equivalent tax/accountancy qualification * Some practical experience in tax compliance or related accounting role (e.g. in practice) * Well‑developed understanding of core Irish tax compliance requirements * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Effective communicator with ability to build positive client and team relationships * Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Word) and willingness to learn tax software **The Offer** * Competitive salary and benefits package * Opportunity to develop and expand technical tax knowledge * Exposure to a broad and varied client portfolio * Supportive team environment with structured learning opportunities * Chance to progress your career within a growing firm

title: Tech Sales & Repair
description: 🔧 Retail Sales \& Tech Repair Specialist 📍 Mullingar \| Full\-Time 🏢 Creative IT We’re looking for an **experienced retail \+ tech repair professional** who can step into a busy shop environment and perform from day one. This is a hands\-on role for someone who is confident with **customers, sales, and repairs** — not a trainee position. 💼 What You’ll Be Doing * Serving customers in\-store with confidence and professionalism * Diagnosing issues and recommending the right solution (repair vs replace) * Carrying out common repairs (phones, laptops, upgrades) * Managing repair bookings and customer expectations * Driving sales — accessories, upgrades, and replacement devices * Keeping the shop running smoothly day\-to\-day 🧠 What We’re Looking For * Proven experience in a **retail and/or tech repair environment** * Strong **sales ability** — you’re comfortable recommending and closing * Solid understanding of **phones, laptops, and common issues** * Ability to work independently and take ownership * Excellent communication and customer handling skills * Reliable, organised, and detail\-focused 🔌 Essential (Non\-Negotiable) * Previous hands\-on experience in **repairs or a similar tech role** * Experience dealing directly with customers in a retail setting 💰 What’s In It For You * Competitive salary (based on experience) * Performance\-based incentives * Opportunity to take on more responsibility as the business grows * Work in a fast\-paced, established local business 🚀 About Creative IT We’re a well\-established business in Mullingar covering **retail, repairs, eCommerce, and IT services** . This role is key to keeping the shop running efficiently — we need someone who can **hit the ground running and take ownership** . This is not a typical “phone shop” role. At Creative IT, we focus on **quality, proper diagnostics, and long\-term customer relationships** — not quick, low\-value fixes. This role will not suit someone used to high\-volume, low\-quality repair environments. We’re looking for someone who takes pride in their work, communicates clearly with customers, and understands the value of doing things properly. 📩 Apply Now Send your CV or message us directly here on LinkedIn.

With sustainability at the forefront, Specsavers Blanchardstown is a community driven store that actively participates with charities and community initiatives alike. **What’s on Offer?** As well as all the support you need and great training and development opportunities, we have a whole load of benefits on offer for you to enjoy. These include: * You can expect to earn up to €35,000 once passed probation and relevant learning \& development modules completed * 30 days annual leave (Includes Bank Holidays) * Full time or part time with weekend flexibility * Professional fees paid * Specsavers Perks – our discounted benefits scheme * Annual subscription to the “Headspace” app * Outstanding clinical and professional development opportunities * Support with IP, other higher accreditations and Pathway * Access to latest clinical technology and equipment * Support with CPD points * Regular team events and team building evenings **What we’re looking for?** If this is still sounding like a bit of you, we do need you to have a few skills to get started as a Trainee Optical Assistant. These include: * Passion for providing excellent customer service and making customers’ lives easier * Great teamwork * A good initiative * Confidence in using IT systems * Excellent communication and listening skills * Prepared to step out of your comfort zone and try something new * Able to receive feedback and be open to development, knowing we’ll be there to support and guide you throughout your process (with your openness and our guidance, your opportunities will be endless). **Find out more** If you’ve got all these skills, we’d love for you to apply, but there’s also some desirable experience that could set you apart from the competition if you have it. **This includes** * Previous experience in a fast\-paced customer service environment * Experience in optics or Audiology business * Basic knowledge/experience of optical and/or Audiology terminology. Please be aware you may be requsted to complete a short video as part of the interview process! **Checked all the boxes? Now’s the perfect time to apply!** Salary: €14\.15 ### **Key information** Location Blanchardstown * Area of interest: Trainee Optical Assistant * Contract type: Permanent Apply by 9th May

title: Trainee Sushi Chef Portmarnock
description: **At Sushi Gourmet** we want everyone to have access to great tasting high quality food that sustain them and the environment. We are a passionate team dedicated to delivering restaurant quality fresh\-made foods at supermarket prices. We are recruiting **Full Time (35 hours) Sushi Chefs** to join our busy team in our location in Sushi Gourmet, Dunnes Stores Shopping center, Wendell Ave, Carrickhill, Portmarnock, Co.Dublin D13 T6P9\. Full training will be provided. This is a day\-time position (6am\-3pm). As our people are our most important asset, and we are seeking the right someone to join our family. **If you are:** Enthusiastic, energetic with the ability to demonstrate initiative Flexible with a ‘can do’ attitude Always happy to help customers with a smile, Able to work as part of a team or on your own whilst maintaining high standards at all times, Excited to produce amazing high quality and sustainable food products Looking to learn new skills, if you have food preparation experience and are confident with knife skills, we can train you to do the rest. **And have:** Pride in your work, constantly looking for ways to improve and continually learning A good understanding of Food Safety \& Health and Safety regulations, ability to follow brand standards and comply with company policies. Reliable transportation for your commute to work early in the morning **And able to:** Prepare fresh ingredients by hand, including meat and raw fish products each shift Accurately labelling products ensuring lawful compliance around food safety and allergen compliance each shift. Be a skilled Multitasker Able to clean the workspace and storerooms as required Be organized Maintaining a safe environment for our customers, our clients and ourselves. An early riser as shifts start at 6am Learn new skills or improve skills already mastered **Then we would like to hear from you!** **In return we offer you** Ability to join a Company which is passionate about sustainability of our products and the planet, while supporting those who make our working lives possible. \- Paid fortnightly \- Free Sushi Gourmet lunch when on shift \- Extra day holiday during the month of your Birthday \- ‘A Day for you’ an extra day of holiday just because you deserve it! \- Monthly bonus when the teams KPI’s are achieved \-Ongoing training throughout the year \- Make a difference fund \-Free uniform \-Stable working environment \- Career opportunities both in UK and Globally. \- Paid holiday (Pro rotated and increasing with LOS) Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Food allowance Experience: * Kitchen: 1 year (preferred) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Transformation Business Analyst
description: **Who We Are** At Cornmarket, we look after the financial wellbeing of public servants and their families. We’ve been focused on public sector employees for over 50 years, learning how to bring our clients the best financial services – from car, home, health and life insurance, to income protection, retirement planning and beyond. This is an exciting time for Cornmarket. We have great ambition and need great people to help us achieve it. If you’re passionate about digital transformation and customer experience, we want to hear from you. **Role Purpose** The Transformation Business Analyst supports the successful delivery of Cornmarket’s multi‑year transformation programme by translating business needs into clear, actionable requirements and ensuring that solutions are aligned to customer, operational, and strategic outcomes. This role plays a critical part in shaping process improvements, validating technology solutions, coordinating business engagement, and ensuring readiness for deployment. With a strong blend of curiosity, analytical discipline, and transformation experience, the Transformation BA acts as a key partner across business, technology, and delivery teams. **Accountabilities of the Role** **Requirements \& Solution Definition** * Lead the elicitation, analysis, and documentation of business requirements, ensuring clarity, traceability, and alignment with transformation outcomes. * Manage detailed functional and non‑functional specifications, ensuring they are robust, complete, and reflect real business needs. * Apply strong curiosity and structured thinking to challenge assumptions, uncover root problems, and propose practical, experience‑enhancing solutions. * Collaborate with business SMEs to define target‑state processes, data needs, and user journeys. **Testing \& Quality Assurance** * Own the creation of UAT strategies, test plans, and test scripts, ensuring they accurately validate requirements and experience intent. * Coordinate User Acceptance Testing, including tester readiness, defect management, business sign‑off, and traceability back to requirements. * Support the evaluation of solution quality throughout development cycles, escalating risks and gaps early. **Deployment \& Business Readiness** * Support deployment planning, including coordination of cutover activities, business communications, and readiness checkpoints. * Ensure impacted teams understand upcoming changes and that business processes / controls are updated. * Partner with Change \& Organisation Design to support training needs and operational adoption. **Stakeholder Engagement** * Act as the primary day‑to‑day liaison between transformation teams and business areas, ensuring clear communication and expectation management. * Facilitate workshops, playback sessions, and requirement walkthroughs to ensure alignment. * Build strong relationships across the organisation and maintain credibility through expertise, reliability, and delivery focus. **Continuous Improvement \& Transformation Support** * Identify opportunities to simplify processes, enhance CX / UX, and standardise ways of working. * Contribute to shaping design principles, working methods, and business rules underpining transformation. * Provide analytical support for business cases, options assessments, and impact assessments as required. **Behavioural Competencies Required** * **Curious \& Proactive:** Naturally inquisitive, always seeking to understand the “why” and “how” behind business challenges. * **Self\-Starter:** Takes initiative, drives work forward independently and thrives in ambiguity. * **Networker:** Builds relationships across all levels and functions, quickly gets to the heart of issues. * **Hands\-On Delivery:** Getting involved in the detail and unblocks issues directly. * **Communication:** Excellent communication skills, able to influence and engage diverse audiences. * **Collaborative:** Empathetic and able to build trust quickly. * **Composure:** Calm, structured, and delivery‑focused under pressure with an ability to handle difficult conversations when required. **Skills, Experience \& Qualifications Required** * 5 Years\+ direct experience in a change management role, ideally with in the Irish Financial Service sector. * Proven experience in transformation or equivalent roles, ideally in a regulated or financial services environment. * Practical knowledge of CRM, workflow, automation tools, and enterprise platforms (e.g. Salesforce advantageous). * Experience working with cross\-functional teams and external partners. * Experience in influencing leadership thinking, not just communicating the facts. * Familiarity with agile methodologies and digital transformation best practices. **What We Can Offer You** * Flexible working from home options * Significant investments in professional development * Annual leave that increases based on service, plus two additional company days * Life leave \& Flexi leave (option to take additional annual leave) * Attractive pension contribution rates * Health insurance or wellness subsidy * Working abroad policy * Fully paid family leave types (maternity/paternity/surrogacy) * Performance based bonus or commission * Income protection \& Life cover * Discounts on financial products * Excellent health \& wellbeing programme * Wellness Wednesdays * Participation in a Company share scheme **Equal Opportunities \& Data Privacy Notice** Cornmarket is committed to building an inclusive workplace environment. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer, striving to create a welcoming environment. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, disability, ethnic background, family status, gender identify or expression, marital status, membership of the Traveller Community, national origin, race, religion, sexual orientation. We are also committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities in the employment application process. To request an accommodation, please contact our Talent Acquisition Team.

title: Vice President (Fiduciary Services)
description: **Date:** 8 Apr 2026 **Location:** Dublin, IE, D01 W213 **Company:** Walkers Global **Overview of Walkers** ----------------------- We are an international firm providing legal, corporate and fiduciary services to the global financial services community. Fluent in multiple jurisdictions and with offices across geographies and time zones, we combine deep expertise with a determination to give our clients the best. Walkers treat everyone as the intelligent grown\-ups they are. We take a grown\-up approach to getting the job done – trusting and empowering our people to deliver consistently, and enabling them to succeed. Diversity is our secret weapon – it’s the sheer breadth of Walkers people that makes us who we are – gathered from across the globe and fluent in languages, jurisdictions and cultures that help us to mirror our clients and keep our own thinking in tune with the world we operate in. WPS sets a “best in class” standard in the provision of corporate services to aircraft leasing and structured finance special purpose vehicles in Ireland. WPS is led by a team of highly skilled professionals drawn from the accounting, audit, company secretarial, compliance and legal fields. The team combines a depth of specialised experience with a focus on consistently delivering high quality results to clients at all times. **The Role** ------------ We are currently seeking an experienced senior Fiduciary professional to join our dynamic and growing transaction management team in Dublin where we focus on serving the structured finance and aviation finance industry. In this role, you will help lead a busy, client facing and professionally qualified team. This is an excellent opportunity to play a key role in a highly regarded and expanding organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous professional development and career development within a collegiate team\-based environment. **Duties \& Responsibilities** ------------------------------ You will have a broad range of responsibilities including: * Manage a number of transaction managers and junior administrators to ensure the delivery of high\-quality client services to a wide range of structured finance and aviation finance special purpose vehicle (SPV) clients; * Act as lead transaction manager for a more complex portfolio of structured finance and aircraft leasing SPVs; * Serve as director to a portfolio of SPVs, being the primary point of contact for all fiduciary and transaction management issues; * Demonstrate complex problem\-solving skills, anticipate issues, lead client board meetings and effectively manage a busy portfolio of clients and team members with competing deadlines and deliverables; * Build and maintain strong client relationships ensuring a high quality, responsive and professional service to our clients at all times; * Lead and manage the delivery of internal regulatory, operational and ad hoc projects and initiatives as required; * Demonstrate awareness and understanding of legal or regulatory developments and proactively seek to ensure that the team is positioned to address those challenges; and * Ensure compliance with internal policies and procedures, while actively contributing to process improvements within the team. **Knowledge \& Experience** --------------------------- * A minimum of ten years' experience in a relevant legal, financial services or corporate administration role is essential, having already progressed into a management role; * Strong academic credentials with a preference for a professional qualification in either the accounting, law or company secretarial profession; * Strong inter\-personal skills and excellent people management skills. An ability to manage, develop and motivate both junior and mid\-level team members is a critical skill for this role; * Energy, enthusiasm and a proactive and solution\-driven attitude; * Understand the intricacies of financial, business and people management; * Ability to think on your feet, show attention to detail and be able to plan and organise work effectively; * Strong organisational, analytical and decision\-making skills with excellent attention to detail; and * A desire to work in a fast\-paced client\-facing environment where delivery of a professional service to both clients and other counterparties is of key importance. **Our Benefits** ---------------- We offer excellent progression opportunities along with a competitive remuneration package and benefits including: * Discretionary annual bonus scheme * 25 days annual leave * Hybrid Working Policy – 3 days in the office * Health insurance cover * Pension scheme * Life Assurance * Income Protection * Taxsaver and Bike to Work schemes * Professional Membership Fees * Social Committee * Employee Assistance Programme * Wellness programme

title: Warehouse Operative
description: **The Opportunity:** -------------------- Executes day\-to\-day warehouse operations according to established guidelines and procedures. Handles routine tasks such as receiving, storing, and shipping materials or products, ensuring that goods are accurately logged and managed in the system. Responsible for maintaining inventory accuracy, reporting discrepancies, and ensuring timely order fulfillment. Operates warehouse equipment, such as forklifts and pallet jacks, to move products efficiently. Maintains a safe and organized work environment while adhering to safety protocols. Resolves common operational issues by applying standard solutions and following established methods. Communicates with team members and supervisors to coordinate tasks and ensure smooth workflow within the warehouse. **Disclaimer:** The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this classification. They are not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required of employees assigned to this position. Avantor is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. **Why Avantor?** **Dare to go further in your career.** Join our global team of 14,000\+ associates whose passion for discovery and determination to overcome challenges relentlessly advances life\-changing science. **The work we do changes people's lives for the better.** It brings new patient treatments and therapies to market, giving a cancer survivor the chance to walk his daughter down the aisle. It enables medical devices that help a little boy hear his mom's voice for the first time. Outcomes such as these create unlimited opportunities for you to contribute your talents, learn new skills and grow your career at Avantor. We are committed to helping you on this journey through our diverse, equitable and inclusive culture which includes learning experiences to support your career growth and success. At Avantor, dare to go further and see how the impact of your contributions set science in motion to create a better world. **Apply today!** **EEO Statement:** We are an Equal Employment/Affirmative Action employer and VEVRAA Federal Contractor. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state/province, or local law. If you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the employment process, please contact us by email at recruiting@avantorsciences.com and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information. Requests for accommodation will be considered on a case\-by\-case basis. Please note that only inquiries concerning a request for reasonable accommodation will be responded to from this email address. **Privacy Policy:** We will use the personal information that you have submitted to us in order to consider your application for the relevant role. Your privacy is important to us. Please click here for our Privacy Policy which explains the purposes for which we will use your personal information and the ways in which we will handle and retain your information. It also explains the rights you have in relation to your information, and how to contact us with any queries or requests. **3rd Party Non\-Solicitation Policy:** By submitting candidates without having been formally assigned on and contracted for a specific job requisition by Avantor, or by failing to comply with the Avantor recruitment process, you forfeit any fee on the submitted candidates, regardless of your usual terms and conditions. Avantor works with a preferred supplier list and will take the initiative to engage with recruitment agencies based on its needs and will not be accepting any form of solicitation.
